Houses.traditional_name: House of the Querent (Helm / House of Self)
governs: The querent (the person asking the question)
The querent's personality, consciousness, and physical body
Any vehicle in which the querent is traveling
The location and weather where the querent is situated
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: Never the House of the Quesited in standard house chart readings. It acts as the anchor representing the querent.
Houses.notes: Mercury rejoices in the helm: speech, carriage, and breath pattern steer outcomes. A martial figure here flags blunt self-assertion; a lunar tail often means the querent already rehearsed a verdict before sitting down. Treat the First as the live camera, not a costume.
Houses.interpretive_essence: The First is the live instrument: pulse, posture, and the story the querent tells while seated. It never holds the quesited in classical house work; it colors every other house by temperament.
common_misreadings: Reading a spouse as First when the chart is clearly seventh-house matter
Assuming Rubeus only means evil rather than heat or secrecy
Treating Populus as empty instead of echoic
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: When the First shows passive multitudes while allies houses blaze, ask whether the narrative is curated for spectators. Pair angular Mars figures with cadent Albus to see if anger is being cooled by counsel.

governs: Investments (excluding speculative ones or real estate)
Specific body parts (mouth, jaw, throat, and food consumed)
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question involves day-to-day finances, financial profits or losses, thefts, and personal belongings.
Houses.notes: Liquidity lives here, not land deeds (those lean Fourth) nor venture dice (Fifth). Classical authors watched conjunctions to the Second for cash literally crossing the table.
example_questions: Will my business make or lose money?
Will the investment be profitable?
Will I get paid for the work I did?
Is this a good time to borrow money?
Is the antique table worth what the seller is asking?
Will the package I shipped arrive safely?
Has my missing watch been stolen?
Will my stolen car be recovered?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Cash flow, movable goods, and the felt sense of enoughness. The Second answers whether resources arrive, depart, or stick in transit.
key_significators: Mouth and throat as intake metaphors
common_misreadings: Routing rent through Second when the lease is a Fourth matter
Calling crypto leverage Second when it behaves like Fifth gambling
Ignoring theft as Second loss
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Amissio on the Second with Acquisitio in the Fifth can mean money dances through risk and back—track the sequence, not the snapshot.

governs: Journeys of less than 200 miles (daily commutes)
Education (preschool through high school) and common skills
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question involves family dynamics (excluding parents), neighbors, local surroundings, media, rumors, or early education.
Houses.notes: The Moon rejoices among letters, cousins, and short wheels. News arrives in packets—SMS, HOA flyer, school portal—not in scrolls.
example_questions: Will my relationship with my sister improve?
Will the neighbors agree to an easement?
Is this a good weekend to drive to the beach?
Should I enroll my child in North Central Elementary?
Is my neighbor George's advice worth following?
Is the news story accurate?
Should I believe the rumors?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Immediate environment and small circuits: kin, neighbors, commutes, and the rumor ecology that feeds decisions.
common_misreadings: Upgrading every group chat to Third when the issue is career-tenth
Confusing deep philosophy (Ninth) with neighborhood beef
Assuming rumor equals fact
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Conjunctio in the Third with Carcer in the Ninth can show paperwork stuck upstream while local chatter runs free—solve the choke point.

governs: Land, real estate, and physical infrastructure
Unknown objects, ancient places, and antiquities
The querent's father, parents in general, and ancestry
The final outcome of any matter
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question involves physical location, real estate, farming, ancestry, or endings. It also always holds the 'Significator of Completion'.
Houses.notes: Angular earth: foundations, endings, fathers, and the chart’s completion speaker. Medieval readers let the Fourth pronounce last when other houses argued.
example_questions: Is the property worth buying?
Is the soil fertile?
Will roses flourish here?
Should I move to another apartment?
Is Springfield a good place to relocate?
Is the table antique or a modern reproduction?
Is the pension fund in reliable hands?
How is my father doing?
How will the present situation turn out in the end?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Roots, soil, dwellings, and how a matter lands when dust settles. Outcomes named here are structural, not theatrical.
common_misreadings: Using Fourth for speculative coins
Calling every basement leak Twelfth when title is Fourth
Skipping completion read when question demands it
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Fortuna Major in the Fourth with Via in the Tenth can show home stability financing a public pivot—weight the heavier angular message.

governs: Crops from perennial or biennial plants
Sexuality, romance, and pleasure (but not marriage)
Parties, sports, and entertainment of all kinds
Bodies of water, fishing, and rain
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question involves fertility, growth, enjoyment, creative pursuits, casual romance, or speculative risks.
Houses.notes: Venus rejoices where joy is grown: flirtation, seedlings, creative risk, and wagers that need sparkle.
example_questions: Will the grape crop be good this year?
Is Janis interested in me sexually?
Is my unborn child a boy or a girl?
How well will my four-year-old cope with a baby brother?
Will the concert be worth the ticket price?
Is next Friday a good night for the party?
Is the new Asian restaurant in town worth a visit?
Will the dress be ready in time for the wedding?
How will fishing be on the lake this weekend?
Will it rain tomorrow?
Will I receive a letter from my grandmother?
Are the claims in the book I'm reading accurate?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Pleasure economies: romance without contract, children and fertility signals, creative flow, and bets that entertain.
common_misreadings: Assigning pregnancy significator away from the person’s natural house without reason
Treating Fifth romance as Seventh marriage
Ignoring water weather omens when literal rain matters
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Puella in the Fifth with Rubeus in the Eighth can tie a fling to shared debt drama—read both timelines.

governs: Practitioners of magic and occultism (other than the querent)
Pets and domestic animals (excluding large livestock)
Illnesses, physical injuries, and health-related routines
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question involves service-people, employees, pets, disease, or routine maintenance.
Houses.notes: Mars rejoices in labor, small animals, and the body’s daily ledger. Illness here is routine disruption, not finality (Eighth handles deeper transformation).
example_questions: Should I hire Phyllis?
Are my employees stealing from the till?
Are the plumbers reliable?
Should I hire a band for the party?
Will I find my lost dog?
Will hogs fetch a good price this fall?
How serious is this illness?
Would it be worthwhile to get a Tarot reading from Marie?
Is this a good time to have dental work done?
Will I catch the flu this winter?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Service loops: jobs, pets, clinicians, and the maintenance calendar that keeps life running.
key_significators: Pets under roof size of goat
common_misreadings: Dumping every burnout into Twelfth instead of checking workload Sixth
Calling every magician Sixth when teaching is Ninth
Confusing acute ER (First body) with chronic grind
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Carcer in the Sixth with Laetitia in the Twelfth can show grind rewarded by private relief—check sleep hygiene.

governs: Conflict, competition, lawsuits, and predator/prey dynamics
Hunting and locating any specific person (known or unknown)
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question involves deep human relationships between two individuals, formal agreements, direct conflicts, or identifying thieves.
Houses.notes: The descendant angle names the opponent, lover, or signatory across the table. Contracts and duels share geometry: two fronts, one line.
example_questions: Does Stanley love me?
Will this relationship last?
Is this a good time to propose marriage?
Should Carol and I form a business partnership?
Should I sign the contract?
Will the Packers win the Super Bowl?
Will India and Pakistan sign a peace treaty?
Will my company's bid on the project be accepted?
Would it be to my advantage to file a lawsuit?
Will the police catch the thief?
Is next weekend a good time for deer hunting?
Will I be able to get in contact with Julie again?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Pair-bonds, open enemies, and mirrored stakes. Seventh is where the querent meets what they cannot swallow alone.
common_misreadings: Forcing a crush into Seventh before reciprocity exists
Reading Packers game as Seventh without identifying the quesited
Ignoring physician-as-seventh in medical charts
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Puer in the Seventh with Albus in the First can show a soft-spoken querent against a loud rival—strategy favors precision, not volume.

governs: Death and everything related to it
Ghosts, spiritual entities, and the occult
Practical magic performed by or on behalf of the querent
Circumstances affecting absent or missing persons
Money, property, or items loaned to another person
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question involves missing people, profound internal shifts, the effects of death, absences, or loaned assets.
Houses.notes: The classical gate of shared pools, absence, and operative sorcery—distinct from Ninth doctrine. Ethical readers decline lifespan prying; still, transformation belongs here.
example_questions: Should I get life insurance?
How seriously should I take Bill's threats?
Is this a good time to make a will?
Is the house actually haunted?
Is the magical working I am planning appropriate?
Is my neighbor's missing daughter all right?
Will I ever get back the book I loaned to Greg?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Other people’s money, voids, hauntings-as-metaphor, and the occult workbench. Ask what is submerged or lent.
common_misreadings: Stuffing university theology into Eighth because it feels spooky
Predicting death detail
Confusing insurance fraud with Second petty theft
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Cauda in the Eighth with Caput in the Second can mark debt closure funding a new cash chapter—sequence matters.

governs: Voyages by water, air, and space
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question involves long-distance travel, spiritual searching, higher education, or divinatory accuracy.
Houses.notes: The Sun rejoices in long roads, publishing, and theologies argued in daylight. Divination’s philosophy sits here; the working altar often reads Eighth.
example_questions: Will I be able to go to Germany this summer?
How congested will the airport be?
Should I pursue my interest in Pagan religion?
Will I be able to get into Harvard?
Is this a good time to go back to school and finish my degree?
Should I sign up for music lessons, or would I be wasting my time?
Does the dream I had last night mean anything?
Should I study geomancy?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Far travel, higher schools, clergy and professors, and the maps we draw to explain the world.
key_significators: Dreams as omen-texts when framed as study
common_misreadings: Labeling every flight Ninth when commute is Third
Putting craft grimoire work here without doctrinal frame
Ignoring literal ocean voyage when chart asks it
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Via in the Ninth with Populus in the Third can show a study abroad rumor mill—verify sources.

governs: Reputation, fame, and position in society
Politics (from local boards to global entities)
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question involves social status, political ambitions, job changes, leadership, or the weather.
Houses.notes: Midheaven angle: the weather report, the boss, the headline name. Mother significations ride here in many lineages alongside career.
example_questions: Should I look for a different job?
Will I get the promotion?
Will I get along with my new boss?
Will the county office approve my building permit?
How is Mom doing these days?
Will Senator Smith win reelection?
Should I run for a seat on the school board?
Will the weather tomorrow be clear and dry?
Should I get a second opinion on the proposed treatment for my heart condition?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Public arc: title, politics, reputation, and the visible mother or authority mirror.
common_misreadings: Confusing freelance brand (First) with institutional role (Tenth)
Mother in Fourth when chart explicitly uses Tenth mother rule
Single headline without aspects
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Fortuna Minor in the Tenth with Tristitia in the Fourth can show a flashy promotion stressing home foundations—negotiate bandwidth.

Houses.astrological_correspondence: Social Life, Community, and Future Vision
governs: The querent's hopes, wishes, and future goals
Any question the querent is unwilling to tell the diviner
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question involves friends, community networks, achieving long-term hopes, helpers, or when the true question is deliberately hidden.
Houses.notes: Jupiter rejoices among friends, benefactors, and the crop that finishes the year. Hidden questions sometimes sit here because hope masks the true ask.
example_questions: How is my old college buddy Bill doing?
Will my friend Sally make good on her promise?
Can I count on the neighborhood association to support me?
Will I achieve my fondest dream?
Will I get a good crop of peas this year?
I don't want to tell you what my question is—can you answer it anyway?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Networks, patrons, and the querent’s forward wishes. Eleventh measures who actually shows when the truck is stuck.
common_misreadings: Reading anonymous forums as Eleventh without querent link
Promising lottery from Jupiter alone
Ignoring benefactor strings attached
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Rubeus in the Eleventh with Populus in the First can show a volatile crowd cheering a querent who is still undecided—probe sincerity.

governs: Secrets, hidden mental health struggles, and the subconscious
Enemies the querent doesn't know about
Harmful magic cast against the querent by another person
Large livestock and wild animals (cattle, horses, donkeys, mules, camels)
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question involves harm, strict limits, debts, hidden enemies, wild animals, or imprisonment.
Houses.notes: Saturn rejoices in what hides: institutions, karmic debts, large beasts, and enemies not yet introduced. Self-undoing is literal when we ghost our own calendar.
example_questions: Will I be able to pay my bills next month?
Will I be sent to prison?
Is Ruth hiding something from me?
Is someone at work trying to get me fired?
Was the poppet in the front yard a joke, or is someone trying to cast a spell on me?
Is this horse worth buying?
Will cattle fetch a good price this year?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Seclusion, large animals, occult attack suspicion, and the subconscious leak that steers the ship.
common_misreadings: Blaming Twelfth for every anxiety when Sixth is workload
Calling every secret enemy Twelfth without evidence
Ignoring large animal market when chart asks ranch
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Laetitia in the Twelfth with Carcer in the Sixth can show hospital joy inside a rigid regimen—read compassion through structure.
